modules.label: autoscaling_group
modules.tooltip: Create or delete AWS AutoScaling Groups (ASGs)

fields.label: access key
fields.description: AWS access key ID. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) or C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(aws_access_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_access_key_id) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: availability zones
fields.description: List of availability zone names in which to create the group. Defaults to all the availability zones in the region if O(vpc_zone_identifier) is not set.

fields.label: aws ca bundle
fields.description: The location of a CA Bundle to use when validating SSL certificates. The C(AWS_CA_BUNDLE) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: aws config
fields.description: A dictionary to modify the botocore configuration. Parameters can be found in the AWS documentation U(https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html#botocore.config.Config).

fields.label: debug botocore endpoint logs
fields.description: Use a C(botocore.endpoint) logger to parse the unique (rather than total) C("resource:action") API calls made during a task, outputing the set to the resource_actions key in the task results. Use the C(aws_resource_action) callback to output to total list made during a playbook. The C(ANSIBLE_DEBUG_BOTOCORE_LOGS) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: decrement desired capacity
fields.description: Support for the O(detach_instances) and O(decrement_desired_capacity) parameters has been deprecated and will be removed in release 14.0.0. The M(amazon.aws.autoscaling_instance) module can be used to attach instances to and detach and detach instances from an AutoScaling Group. Indicates whether the AutoScalingGroup decrements the desired capacity value by the number of instances detached.

fields.label: default cooldown
fields.description: The number of seconds after a scaling activity completes before another can begin.

fields.label: desired capacity
fields.description: Desired number of instances in group, if unspecified then the current group value will be used.

fields.label: detach instances
fields.description: Support for the O(detach_instances) parameter has been deprecated and will be removed in release 14.0.0. The M(amazon.aws.autoscaling_instance) module can be used to attach instances to and detach and detach instances from an AutoScaling Group. Removes one or more instances from the specified AutoScalingGroup. If O(decrement_desired_capacity) flag is not set, new instance(s) are launched to replace the detached instance(s). If a Classic Load Balancer is attached to the AutoScalingGroup, the instances are also deregistered from the load balancer. If there are target groups attached to the AutoScalingGroup, the instances are also deregistered from the target groups.

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: health check period
fields.description: Length of time in seconds after a new EC2 instance comes into service that Auto Scaling starts checking its health.

fields.label: health check type
fields.description: The service you want the health status from, Amazon EC2 or Elastic Load Balancer.

fields.label: launch config name
fields.description: Name of the Launch configuration to use for the group. See the M(community.aws.autoscaling_launch_config) module for managing these. Exactly one of O(launch_config_name) or O(launch_template) must be provided when creating a new AutoScaling Group. B(Note) Amazon has deprecated support for AutoScaling Launch Configurations in favour of EC2 Launch Templates.  See U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/launch-configurations.html) for more information

fields.label: lc check
fields.description: Support for the O(detach_instances) and O(lc_check) parameters has been deprecated and will be removed in release 14.0.0. The M(amazon.aws.autoscaling_instance) module can be used to attach instances to and detach and detach instances from an AutoScaling Group. Check to make sure instances that are being replaced with O(replace_instances) do not already have the current launch config.

fields.label: load balancers
fields.description: List of ELB names to use for the group. Use for classic load balancers.

fields.label: lt check
fields.description: Support for the O(detach_instances) and O(lt_check) parameters has been deprecated and will be removed in release 14.0.0. The M(amazon.aws.autoscaling_instance) module can be used to attach instances to and detach and detach instances from an AutoScaling Group. Check to make sure instances that are being replaced with O(replace_instances) do not already have the current O(launch_template) or O(launch_template) O(launch_template.version).

fields.label: max instance lifetime
fields.description: The maximum amount of time, in seconds, that an instance can be in service. Maximum instance lifetime must be equal to V(0), between V(604800) and V(31536000) seconds (inclusive), or not specified. Value of V(0) removes lifetime restriction.

fields.label: max size
fields.description: Maximum number of instances in group, if unspecified then the current group value will be used.

fields.label: metrics collection
fields.description: Enable ASG metrics collection.

fields.label: metrics granularity
fields.description: When O(metrics_collection=true) this will determine the granularity of metrics collected by CloudWatch.

fields.label: metrics list
fields.description: List of autoscaling metrics to collect when O(metrics_collection=true).

fields.label: min size
fields.description: Minimum number of instances in group, if unspecified then the current group value will be used.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Unique name for group to be created or deleted.

fields.label: notification topic
fields.description: A SNS topic ARN to send auto scaling notifications to.

fields.label: notification types
fields.description: A list of auto scaling events to trigger notifications on.

fields.label: placement group
fields.description: Physical location of your cluster placement group created in Amazon EC2.

fields.label: profile
fields.description: A named AWS profile to use for authentication. See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: protected from scale in
fields.description: If V(true), new instances will have scale-in protection enabled when added to the AutoScaling Group. Defaults to V(false) when creating a new AutoScaling Group.

fields.label: purge tags
fields.description: If V(true), existing tags will be purged from the resource to match exactly what is defined by O(tags) parameter. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The AWS region to use. For global services such as IAM, Route53 and CloudFront, I(region) is ignored. The C(AWS_REGION) environment variable may also be used. See the Amazon AWS documentation for more information U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#ec2_region).

fields.label: replace all instances
fields.description: Support for the O(replace_all_instances) parameter has been deprecated and will be removed in release 14.0.0. The M(amazon.aws.autoscaling_instance_refresh) module can be used to perform an automated replacement of instances. In a rolling fashion, replace all instances that used the old launch configuration with one from the new launch configuration. It increases the ASG size by O(replace_batch_size), waits for the new instances to be up and running. After that, it terminates a batch of old instances, waits for the replacements, and repeats, until all old instances are replaced. Once that's done the ASG size is reduced back to the expected size.

fields.label: replace batch size
fields.description: Support for the O(replace_all_instances) and O(replace_batch_size) parameters has been deprecated and will be removed in release 14.0.0. The M(amazon.aws.autoscaling_instance_refresh) module can be used to perform an automated replacement of instances. Number of instances you'd like to replace at a time.  Used with O(replace_all_instances).

fields.label: replace instances
fields.description: Support for the O(replace_instances) parameter has been deprecated and will be removed in release 14.0.0. The M(amazon.aws.autoscaling_instance) module can be used to terminate instances attached to an AutoScaling Group. List of instance ids belonging to the named AutoScalingGroup that you would like to terminate and be replaced with instances matching the current launch configuration.

fields.label: secret key
fields.description: AWS secret access key. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or C(AWS_SECRET_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(secret_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_secret_access_key) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: session token
fields.description: AWS STS session token for use with temporary credentials. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SESSION_TOKEN) environment variable may also be used. The I(session_token) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Register or deregister the instance.

fields.label: suspend processes
fields.description: A list of scaling processes to suspend. Valid values include: V(Launch), V(Terminate), V(HealthCheck), V(ReplaceUnhealthy), V(AZRebalance), V(AlarmNotification), V(ScheduledActions), V(AddToLoadBalancer) Full documentation of valid values can be found in the AWS documentation: U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/as-suspend-resume-processes.html)

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A list of tags to add to the Auto Scale Group. Optional key is V(propagate_at_launch), which defaults to V(true). When V(propagate_at_launch) is V(true) the tags will be propagated to the Instances created.

fields.label: target group arns
fields.description: List of target group ARNs to use for the group. Use for application load balancers.

fields.label: termination policies
fields.description: An ordered list of criteria used for selecting instances to be removed from the Auto Scaling group when reducing capacity. Using O(termination_policies=Default) when modifying an existing AutoScalingGroup will result in the existing policy being retained instead of changed to V(Default). Valid values include: V(Default), V(OldestInstance), V(NewestInstance), V(OldestLaunchConfiguration), V(ClosestToNextInstanceHour) Full documentation of valid values can be found in the AWS documentation: U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/ec2/userguide/as-instance-termination.html#custom-termination-policy)

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

fields.label: vpc zone identifier
fields.description: List of VPC subnets to use

fields.label: wait for instances
fields.description: Wait for the ASG instances to be in a ready state before exiting.  If instances are behind an ELB, it will wait until the ELB determines all instances have a lifecycle_state of  "InService" and  a health_status of "Healthy".

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: How long to wait for instances to become viable when replaced.  If you experience the error "Waited too long for ELB instances to be healthy", try increasing this value.

modules.label: autoscaling_group_info
modules.tooltip: Gather information about EC2 Auto Scaling Groups (ASGs) in AWS

fields.label: access key
fields.description: AWS access key ID. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) or C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(aws_access_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_access_key_id) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: aws ca bundle
fields.description: The location of a CA Bundle to use when validating SSL certificates. The C(AWS_CA_BUNDLE) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: aws config
fields.description: A dictionary to modify the botocore configuration. Parameters can be found in the AWS documentation U(https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html#botocore.config.Config).

fields.label: debug botocore endpoint logs
fields.description: Use a C(botocore.endpoint) logger to parse the unique (rather than total) C("resource:action") API calls made during a task, outputing the set to the resource_actions key in the task results. Use the C(aws_resource_action) callback to output to total list made during a playbook. The C(ANSIBLE_DEBUG_BOTOCORE_LOGS) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The prefix or name of the auto scaling group(s) you are searching for. Note: This is a regular expression match with implicit '^' (beginning of string). Append '$' for a complete name match.

fields.label: profile
fields.description: A named AWS profile to use for authentication. See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The AWS region to use. For global services such as IAM, Route53 and CloudFront, I(region) is ignored. The C(AWS_REGION) environment variable may also be used. See the Amazon AWS documentation for more information U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#ec2_region).

fields.label: secret key
fields.description: AWS secret access key. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or C(AWS_SECRET_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(secret_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_secret_access_key) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: session token
fields.description: AWS STS session token for use with temporary credentials. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SESSION_TOKEN) environment variable may also be used. The I(session_token) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A dictionary/hash of tags in the format { tag1_name: 'tag1_value', tag2_name: 'tag2_value' } to match against the auto scaling group(s) you are searching for.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

modules.label: autoscaling_instance
modules.tooltip: manage instances associated with AWS AutoScaling Groups (ASGs)

fields.label: access key
fields.description: AWS access key ID. The I(aws_access_key_id) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: aws ca bundle
fields.description: The location of a CA Bundle to use when validating SSL certificates. The C(AWS_CA_BUNDLE) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: aws config
fields.description: A dictionary to modify the botocore configuration. Parameters can be found in the AWS documentation U(https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html#botocore.config.Config).

fields.label: debug botocore endpoint logs
fields.description: Use a C(botocore.endpoint) logger to parse the unique (rather than total) C("resource:action") API calls made during a task, outputing the set to the resource_actions key in the task results. Use the C(aws_resource_action) callback to output to total list made during a playbook. The C(ANSIBLE_DEBUG_BOTOCORE_LOGS) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: decrement desired capacity
fields.description: When O(decrement_desired_capacity=True), detaching instances, terminating instances, or placing instances in standby mode will decrement the desired capacity of the AutoScaling Group

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: group name
fields.description: Name of the AutoScaling Group to manage.

fields.label: health
fields.description: Sets the health of an instance to a specific state.

fields.label: instance ids
fields.description: The IDs of the EC2 instances. Required if O(state) is one of V(standby), V(detached), V(terminated).

fields.label: profile
fields.description: A named AWS profile to use for authentication. See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: protection
fields.description: Sets the scale-in protection attribute.

fields.label: purge instances
fields.description: Ignored unless O(state=present) or O(state=attached). If O(purge_instances=True), any instances not in O(instance_ids) will be scheduled for B(termination). B(Note:) Instances will be scheduled for termination B(after) any new instances are added to the AutoScaling Group and, if O(wait=True) and they will be terminated B(after) the new instances have reached the expected state.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The AWS region to use. For global services such as IAM, Route53 and CloudFront, I(region) is ignored. The C(AWS_REGION) environment variable may also be used. See the Amazon AWS documentation for more information U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#ec2_region).

fields.label: respect grace period
fields.description: Set O(respect_grace_period=False) to ignore the grace period associated with the AutoScaling group when modifying the O(health). Ignored unless O(health) is set. AWS defaults to respecting the grace period when modifying the health state of an instance.

fields.label: secret key
fields.description: AWS secret access key. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or C(AWS_SECRET_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(secret_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_secret_access_key) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: session token
fields.description: AWS STS session token for use with temporary credentials. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SESSION_TOKEN) environment variable may also be used. The I(session_token) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: state
fields.description: The expected state of the instances. V(present) - The instance(s) should be attached to the AutoScaling Group and in service. V(attached) - The instance(s) should be attached to the AutoScaling Group. Instances in Standby will remain in standby. V(standby) - The instance(s) should be placed into standby. Instances must already be part of the AutoScaling Group. V(detached) - The instance(s) will be detached from the AutoScaling Group. V(terminated) - The instance(s) will be terminated. By default terminated instances will be replaced with new instances, to reduce the desired capacity at the same time as terminating instances set O(decrement_desired_capacity=True). B(Note:) When adding instances to an AutoScaling Group or returning instances to service from standby, the desired capacity is B(always) incremented.  If the total number of instances would exceed the maximum size of the group then the operation will fail.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: When O(wait=True) will wait for instances to reach the requested state before returning.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: Maximum time to wait for instances to reach the desired state.

fields.label: strategy
fields.description: The strategy to use for the instance refresh. The only valid value is V(Rolling). A rolling update is an update that is applied to all instances in an Auto Scaling group until all instances have been updated. A rolling update can fail due to failed health checks or if instances are on standby or are protected from scale in. If the rolling update process fails, any instances that were already replaced are not rolled back to their previous configuration.

fields.description: A dictionary to modify the botocore configuration. Parameters can be found in the AWS documentation U(https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html#botocore.config.Config).

fields.label: backoff delay
fields.description: Number of seconds to wait for the next retry.

fields.label: backoff max delay
fields.description: Maximum amount of time to wait between retries.

fields.label: backoff retries
fields.description: Number of times to retry operation. AWS API throttling mechanism fails CloudFormation module so we have to retry a couple of times.

fields.label: capabilities
fields.description: Specify capabilities that stack template contains. Valid values are V(CAPABILITY_IAM), V(CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM) and V(CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND).

fields.label: changeset name
fields.description: Name given to the changeset when creating a changeset. Only used when O(create_changeset=true). By default a name prefixed with Ansible-STACKNAME is generated based on input parameters. See the AWS Change Sets docs for more information U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/using-cfn-updating-stacks-changesets.html)

fields.label: create changeset
fields.description: If stack already exists create a changeset instead of directly applying changes.  See the AWS Change Sets docs U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/using-cfn-updating-stacks-changesets.html). B(WARNING:) if the stack does not exist, it will be created without changeset. If O(state=absent), the stack will be deleted immediately with no changeset.

fields.label: create timeout
fields.description: The amount of time (in minutes) that can pass before the stack status becomes V(CREATE_FAILED).

fields.label: debug botocore endpoint logs
fields.description: Use a C(botocore.endpoint) logger to parse the unique (rather than total) C("resource:action") API calls made during a task, outputing the set to the resource_actions key in the task results. Use the C(aws_resource_action) callback to output to total list made during a playbook. The C(ANSIBLE_DEBUG_BOTOCORE_LOGS) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: disable rollback
fields.description: If a stacks fails to form, rollback will remove the stack.

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: events limit
fields.description: Maximum number of CloudFormation events to fetch from a stack when creating or updating it.

fields.label: notification arns
fields.description: A comma separated list of Simple Notification Service (SNS) topic ARNs to publish stack related events.

fields.label: on create failure
fields.description: Action to take upon failure of stack creation. Incompatible with the O(disable_rollback) option.

fields.label: profile
fields.description: A named AWS profile to use for authentication. See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The AWS region to use. For global services such as IAM, Route53 and CloudFront, I(region) is ignored. The C(AWS_REGION) environment variable may also be used. See the Amazon AWS documentation for more information U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#ec2_region).

fields.label: role arn
fields.description: The role that AWS CloudFormation assumes to create the stack. See the AWS CloudFormation Service Role docs U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/using-iam-servicerole.html)

fields.label: secret key
fields.description: AWS secret access key. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or C(AWS_SECRET_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(secret_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_secret_access_key) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: session token
fields.description: AWS STS session token for use with temporary credentials. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SESSION_TOKEN) environment variable may also be used. The I(session_token) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: stack name
fields.description: Name of the CloudFormation stack.

fields.label: stack policy
fields.description: The path of the file containing the CloudFormation stack policy. A policy cannot be removed once placed, but it can be modified. for instance, allow all updates U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/protect-stack-resources.html#d0e9051)

fields.label: stack policy body
fields.description: The CloudFormation stack policy in JSON. A policy cannot be removed once placed, but it can be modified. for instance, allow all updates U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/protect-stack-resources.html#d0e9051)

fields.label: stack policy on update body
fields.description: The body of the cloudformation stack policy only applied during this update.

fields.label: state
fields.description: If O(state=present), stack will be created. If O(state=present) and if stack exists and template has changed, it will be updated. If O(state=absent), stack will be removed.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: Dictionary of tags to associate with stack and its resources during stack creation. Can be updated later, updating tags removes previous entries.

fields.label: template
fields.description: The local path of the CloudFormation template. This must be the full path to the file, relative to the working directory. If using roles this may look like V(roles/cloudformation/files/cloudformation-example.json). If O(state=present) and the stack does not exist yet, either O(template), O(template_body) or O(template_url) must be specified (but only one of them). If O(state=present), the stack does exist, and neither O(template), O(template_body) nor O(template_url) are specified, the previous template will be reused. The O(template) parameter has been deprecated and will be remove in a release after 2026-05-01. It is recommended to use O(template_body) with the P(ansible.builtin.template#lookup) lookup plugin.

fields.label: template body
fields.description: Template body. Use this to pass in the actual body of the CloudFormation template. If O(state=present) and the stack does not exist yet, either O(template), O(template_body) or O(template_url) must be specified (but only one of them). If O(state=present), the stack does exist, and neither O(template), O(template_body) nor O(template_url) are specified, the previous template will be reused.

fields.label: template parameters
fields.description: A list of hashes of all the template variables for the stack. The value can be a string or a dict. Dict can be used to set additional template parameter attributes like UsePreviousValue (see example).

fields.label: template url
fields.description: Location of file containing the template body. The URL must point to a template (max size 307,200 bytes) located in an S3 bucket in the same region as the stack. If O(state=present) and the stack does not exist yet, either O(template), O(template_body) or O(template_url) must be specified (but only one of them). If O(state=present), the stack does exist, and neither O(template), O(template_body) nor O(template_url) are specified, the previous template will be reused.

fields.label: termination protection
fields.description: Enable or disable termination protection on the stack.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

fields.description: The name or id of the CloudFormation stack. Gathers information on all stacks by default.

fields.label: cloudwatch logs log group arn
fields.description: A full ARN specifying a valid CloudWatch log group to which CloudTrail logs will be delivered. The log group should already exist. See U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/send-cloudtrail-events-to-cloudwatch-logs.html). Required when O(cloudwatch_logs_role_arn).

fields.label: cloudwatch logs role arn
fields.description: Specifies a full ARN for an IAM role that assigns the proper permissions for CloudTrail to create and write to the log group. See U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/send-cloudtrail-events-to-cloudwatch-logs.html). Required when O(cloudwatch_logs_log_group_arn).

fields.label: debug botocore endpoint logs
fields.description: Use a C(botocore.endpoint) logger to parse the unique (rather than total) C("resource:action") API calls made during a task, outputing the set to the resource_actions key in the task results. Use the C(aws_resource_action) callback to output to total list made during a playbook. The C(ANSIBLE_DEBUG_BOTOCORE_LOGS) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: enable log file validation
fields.description: Specifies whether log file integrity validation is enabled. CloudTrail will create a hash for every log file delivered and produce a signed digest file that can be used to ensure log files have not been tampered.

fields.label: enable logging
fields.description: Start or stop the CloudTrail logging. If stopped the trail will be paused and will not record events or deliver log files.

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: include global events
fields.description: Record API calls from global services such as IAM and STS.

fields.label: is multi region trail
fields.description: Specify whether the trail belongs only to one region or exists in all regions.

fields.label: kms key id
fields.description: Specifies the KMS key ID to use to encrypt the logs delivered by CloudTrail. This also has the effect of enabling log file encryption. The value can be an alias name prefixed by "alias/", a fully specified ARN to an alias, a fully specified ARN to a key, or a globally unique identifier. Encryption can be disabled by setting O(kms_key_id=""). See U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/encrypting-cloudtrail-log-files-with-aws-kms.html).

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name for the CloudTrail. Names are unique per-region unless the CloudTrail is a multi-region trail, in which case it is unique per-account.

fields.label: profile
fields.description: A named AWS profile to use for authentication. See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: purge tags
fields.description: If O(purge_tags=true) and O(tags) is set, existing tags will be purged from the resource to match exactly what is defined by O(tags) parameter. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified, even if O(purge_tags=True). Tag keys beginning with V(aws:) are reserved by Amazon and can not be modified.  As such they will be ignored for the purposes of the O(purge_tags) parameter.  See the Amazon documentation for more information U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws_tagging.html#tag-conventions).

fields.label: region
fields.description: The AWS region to use. For global services such as IAM, Route53 and CloudFront, I(region) is ignored. The C(AWS_REGION) environment variable may also be used. See the Amazon AWS documentation for more information U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#ec2_region).

fields.label: s3 bucket name
fields.description: An existing S3 bucket where CloudTrail will deliver log files. This bucket should exist and have the proper policy. See U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/aggregating_logs_regions_bucket_policy.html). Required when O(state=present).

fields.label: s3 key prefix
fields.description: S3 Key prefix for delivered log files. A trailing slash is not necessary and will be removed.

fields.label: secret key

See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or C(AWS_SECRET_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(secret_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_secret_access_key) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: session token
fields.description: AWS STS session token for use with temporary credentials. The C(AWS_SESSION_TOKEN) environment variable may also be used. The I(session_token) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: sns topic name
fields.description: SNS Topic name to send notifications to when a log file is delivered.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Add or remove CloudTrail configuration. The following states have been preserved for backwards compatibility: O(state=enabled) and O(state=disabled). O(state=enabled) is equivalet to O(state=present). O(state=disabled) is equivalet to O(state=absent).

fields.description: A dictionary representing the tags to be applied to the resource. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified.

fields.label: validate certs

fields.label: state
fields.description: Register or deregister an AMI.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A dictionary representing the tags to be applied to the resource. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified.

fields.label: tpm support
fields.description: Set to V(v2.0) to enable Trusted Platform Module (TPM) support. If the image is configured for NitroTPM support, the value is V(v2.0). Requires O(boot_mode) to be set to V(uefi). Requires an instance type that is compatible with Nitro. See the AWS documentation for more detail U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/nitrotpm.html).

fields.label: uefi data
fields.description: Base64 representation of the non-volatile UEFI variable store. See the AWS documentation for more detail U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/uefi-secure-boot.html).

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

fields.label: virtualization type
fields.description: The virtualization type of the image to register.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Wait for the AMI to be in state 'available' before returning.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

The C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) or C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(aws_access_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_access_key_id) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: executable users
fields.description: Filter images by users with explicit launch permissions. Valid options are an AWS account ID, self, or all (public AMIs).

fields.description: A dict of filters to apply. Each dict item consists of a filter key and a filter value. See U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_DescribeImages.html) for possible filters. Filter names and values are case sensitive.

fields.label: image ids
fields.description: One or more image IDs.

fields.label: owners
fields.description: Filter the images by the owner. Valid options are an AWS account ID, self, or an AWS owner alias ( amazon | aws-marketplace | microsoft ).

fields.label: profile

The C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) or C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(aws_access_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_access_key_id) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: asset ids
fields.description: The IDs of the Outpost hardware assets on which to allocate the Dedicated Hosts. Targeting specific hardware assets on an Outpost can help to minimize latency between your workloads. This parameter is supported only if you specify O(outpost_arn). If you specify this parameter, you can omit O(quantity). If you specify both O(asset_ids) and O(quantity), then the value for O(quantity) must be equal to the number of O(asset_ids) specified.

fields.label: auto placement
fields.description: Indicates whether the host accepts any untargeted instance launches that match its instance type configuration, or if it only accepts Host tenancy instance launches that specify its unique host ID.

fields.label: availability zone
fields.description: The Availability Zone in which to allocate the Dedicated Host. Required if O(state=present).

fields.label: aws ca bundle
fields.description: The location of a CA Bundle to use when validating SSL certificates. The C(AWS_CA_BUNDLE) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: aws config
fields.description: A dictionary to modify the botocore configuration. Parameters can be found in the AWS documentation U(https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html#botocore.config.Config).

fields.label: client token
fields.description: case-sensitive identifier that you provide to ensure the idempotency of the request.

fields.label: debug botocore endpoint logs
fields.description: Use a C(botocore.endpoint) logger to parse the unique (rather than total) C("resource:action") API calls made during a task, outputing the set to the resource_actions key in the task results. fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: host id
fields.description: The IDs of the Dedicated Hosts to release or modify

fields.label: host maintenance
fields.description: Indicates whether to enable or disable host maintenance for the Dedicated Host.

fields.label: host recovery
fields.description: Indicates whether to enable or disable host recovery for the Dedicated Host. Host recovery is disabled by default.

fields.label: instance family
fields.description: Specifies the instance family to be supported by the Dedicated Hosts. If you specify an instance family, the Dedicated Hosts support multiple instance types within that instance family. At least one of O(instance_family) or O(instance_type) must be specified when allocating dedicated host. Mutually exclusive with paramter O(instance_type).

fields.label: instance type
fields.description: Specifies the instance type to be supported by the Dedicated Hosts. If you specify an instance type, the Dedicated Hosts support instances of the specified instance type only. At least one of O(instance_family) or O(instance_type) must be specified when allocating dedicated host. Mutually exclusive with paramter O(instance_family).

fields.label: lookup
fields.description: Look up EC2 dedicated host by either O(tags) or by O(host_id). If O(lookup=tag) and O(tags) is not specified then no lookup for an existing dedicated host is performed and a new dedicated host will be created. When using O(lookup=tag), multiple matches being found will result in a failure and no changes will be made. To change the tags of a dedicated host, use O(lookup=host_id).

fields.label: outpost arn
fields.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Web Services Outpost on which to allocate the Dedicated Host.

fields.label: profile
fields.description: A named AWS profile to use for authentication. See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: purge tags
fields.description: If O(purge_tags=true) and O(tags) is set, existing tags will be purged from the resource to match exactly what is defined by O(tags) parameter. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified, even if O(purge_tags=True). Tag keys beginning with V(aws:) are reserved by Amazon and can not be modified.  As such they will be ignored for the purposes of the O(purge_tags) parameter.  See the Amazon documentation for more information U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws_tagging.html#tag-conventions).

fields.label: quantity
fields.description: The number of Dedicated Hosts to allocate to your account with these parameters. Required when O(state=present). If you are allocating the Dedicated Hosts on an Outpost, and you specify O(asset_ids), you can omit this parameter. If you specify both O(asset_ids) and O(quantity), then the value that you specify for O(quantity) must be equal to the number of O(asset_ids) specified.

Use the C(aws_resource_action) callback to output to total list made during a playbook. The C(ANSIBLE_DEBUG_BOTOCORE_LOGS) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: delete on termination
fields.description: Delete the interface when the instance it is attached to is terminated. You can only specify this flag when the interface is being modified, not on creation.

fields.label: description
fields.description: Optional description of the ENI.

fields.label: device index
fields.description: The index of the device for the network interface attachment on the instance.

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: eni id
fields.description: The ID of the ENI (to modify). If O(eni_id=None) and O(state=present), a new ENI will be created.

fields.label: force detach
fields.description: Force detachment of the interface. This applies either when explicitly detaching the interface by setting O(instance_id=None) or when deleting an interface with O(state=absent).

fields.label: instance id
fields.description: Instance ID that you wish to attach ENI to.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Name for the ENI. This will create a tag with the key V(Name) and the value assigned here. This can be used in conjunction with O(subnet_id) as another means of identifiying a network interface. AWS does not enforce unique V(Name) tags, so duplicate names are possible if you configure it that way. If that is the case, you will need to provide other identifying information such as O(private_ip_address) or O(eni_id).

fields.label: private ip address
fields.description: Private IP address.

fields.label: profile
fields.description: A named AWS profile to use for authentication. See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: purge secondary private ip addresses
fields.description: To be used with O(secondary_private_ip_addresses) to determine whether or not to remove any secondary IP addresses other than those specified. Set O(secondary_private_ip_addresses=[]) to purge all secondary addresses.

fields.label: purge tags
fields.description: If O(purge_tags=true) and O(tags) is set, existing tags will be purged from the resource to match exactly what is defined by O(tags) parameter. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified, even if O(purge_tags=True). Tag keys beginning with V(aws:) are reserved by Amazon and can not be modified.  As such they will be ignored for the purposes of the O(purge_tags) parameter.  See the Amazon documentation for more information U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws_tagging.html#tag-conventions).

fields.label: secondary private ip address count

fields.label: secondary private ip addresses

The C(AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or C(AWS_SECRET_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(secret_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_secret_access_key) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

fields.description: Number of instances to launch. Setting this value will result in always launching new instances. Mutually exclusive with O(exact_count).

fields.label: cpu credit specification
fields.description: For T series instances, choose whether to allow increased charges to buy CPU credits if the default pool is depleted. Choose V(unlimited) to enable buying additional CPU credits.

fields.label: debug botocore endpoint logs

fields.label: ebs optimized
fields.description: Whether instance is should use optimized EBS volumes, see U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSOptimized.html).

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: exact count
fields.description: An integer value which indicates how many instances that match the O(filters) parameter should be running. Instances are either created or terminated based on this value. If termination takes place, least recently created instances will be terminated based on Launch Time. Mutually exclusive with O(count), O(instance_ids).

fields.label: filters
fields.description: A dict of filters to apply when deciding whether existing instances match and should be altered. Each dict item consists of a filter key and a filter value. See U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_DescribeInstances.html). for possible filters. Filter names and values are case sensitive. By default, instances are filtered for counting by their "Name" tag, base AMI, state (running, by default), and subnet ID. Any queryable filter can be used. Good candidates are specific tags, SSH keys, or security groups.

fields.label: hibernation options
fields.description: Indicates whether an instance is enabled for hibernation. Refer U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/hibernating-prerequisites.html) for Hibernation prerequisits.

fields.label: iam instance profile
fields.description: The ARN or name of an EC2-enabled IAM instance profile to be used. If a name is not provided in ARN format then the ListInstanceProfiles permission must also be granted. U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/APIReference/API_ListInstanceProfiles.html) If no full ARN is provided, the role with a matching name will be used from the active AWS account.

fields.label: image id
fields.description: I(ami) ID to use for the instance. One of O(image) or O(image_id) are required when instance is not already present. This is an alias for O(image.id).

fields.label: instance ids
fields.description: If you specify one or more instance IDs, only instances that have the specified IDs are returned. Mutually exclusive with O(exact_count).

fields.label: instance initiated shutdown behavior
fields.description: Whether to stop or terminate an instance upon shutdown.

fields.label: instance type
fields.description: Instance type to use for the instance, see U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instance-types.html). At least one of O(instance_type) or O(launch_template) must be specificed when launching an instance. When the instance is present and the O(instance_type) specified value is different from the current value, the instance will be stopped and the instance type will be updated.

fields.label: key name
fields.description: Name of the SSH access key to assign to the instance - must exist in the region the instance is created. Use M(amazon.aws.ec2_key) to manage SSH keys.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The Name tag for the instance.

fields.label: placement group

fields.label: secret key

The C(AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or C(AWS_SECRET_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(secret_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_secret_access_key) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: security group
fields.description: A security group ID or name. Mutually exclusive with O(security_groups). Mutually exclusive with O(network_interfaces_ids).

fields.label: security groups
fields.description: A list of security group IDs or names (strings). Mutually exclusive with O(security_group). Mutually exclusive with O(network_interfaces_ids).

fields.label: session token
fields.description: AWS STS session token for use with temporary credentials. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SESSION_TOKEN) environment variable may also be used. The I(session_token) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: source dest check
fields.description: Controls whether source/destination checking is enabled on the interface.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Goal state for the instances. O(state=present): ensures instances exist, but does not guarantee any state (e.g. running). Newly-launched instances will be run by EC2. O(state=running): O(state=present) + ensures the instances are running. O(state=started): O(state=running) + waits for EC2 status checks to report OK if O(wait=true). O(state=stopped): ensures an existing instance is stopped. O(state=rebooted): convenience alias for O(state=stopped) immediately followed by O(state=running). O(state=restarted): convenience alias for O(state=stopped) immediately followed by O(state=started). O(state=terminated): ensures an existing instance is terminated. O(state=absent): alias for O(state=terminated).

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A dictionary representing the tags to be applied to the resource. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified.

fields.label: tenancy
fields.description: What type of tenancy to allow an instance to use. Default is V(shared) tenancy. Dedicated tenancy will incur additional charges. This field is deprecated and will be removed in a release after 2025-12-01, use O(placement) instead.

fields.label: termination protection

fields.label: user data

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

fields.label: volumes
fields.description: A list of block device mappings, by default this will always use the AMI root device so the volumes option is primarily for adding more storage. A mapping contains the (optional) keys V(device_name), V(virtual_name), V(ebs.volume_type), V(ebs.volume_size), V(ebs.kms_key_id), V(ebs.snapshot_id), V(ebs.iops), and V(ebs.delete_on_termination). For more information about each parameter, see U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_BlockDeviceMapping.html).

fields.label: vpc subnet id

If used along with O(network), O(vpc_subnet_id) is used as a fallback to prevent errors when O(network.subnet_id) is not specified.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Whether or not to wait for the desired O(state) (use O(wait_timeout) to customize this).

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: How long to wait (in seconds) for the instance to finish booting/terminating.

fields.label: default version
fields.description: Which version should be the default when users spin up new instances based on this template? By default, the latest version will be made the default.

fields.label: disable api termination
fields.description: This helps protect instances from accidental termination. If set to V(true), you can't terminate the instance using the Amazon EC2 console, CLI, or API.

fields.label: ebs optimized
fields.description: Indicates whether the instance is optimized for Amazon EBS I/O. This optimization provides dedicated throughput to Amazon EBS and an optimized configuration stack to provide optimal Amazon EBS I/O performance. This optimization isn't available with all instance types. Additional usage charges apply when using an EBS-optimized instance.

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: iam instance profile
fields.description: The name or ARN of an IAM instance profile. Requires permissions to describe existing instance roles to confirm ARN is properly formed.

fields.label: image id
fields.description: The AMI ID to use for new instances launched with this template. This value is region-dependent since AMIs are not global resources.

fields.label: instance initiated shutdown behavior
fields.description: Indicates whether an instance stops or terminates when you initiate shutdown from the instance using the operating system shutdown command.

fields.label: instance type
fields.description: The instance type, such as V(c5.2xlarge). For a full list of instance types, see U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instance-types.html).

fields.label: kernel id
fields.description: The ID of the kernel. We recommend that you use PV-GRUB instead of kernels and RAM disks. For more information, see U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/UserProvidedkernels.html)

fields.label: key name
fields.description: The name of the key pair. You can create a key pair using M(amazon.aws.ec2_key). If you do not specify a key pair, you can't connect to the instance unless you choose an AMI that is configured to allow users another way to log in.

fields.label: profile
fields.description: A named AWS profile to use for authentication. See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: purge tags

As such they will be ignored for the purposes of the O(purge_tags) parameter.  See the Amazon documentation for more information U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws_tagging.html#tag-conventions).

fields.label: ram disk id

fields.label: security group ids

fields.label: security groups
fields.description: A list of security group names (Default VPC or EC2-Classic) that the new instances will be added to. For any VPC other than Default, you must use O(security_group_ids).

fields.label: session token

The C(AWS_SESSION_TOKEN) environment variable may also be used. The I(session_token) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: source version
fields.description: The version number of the launch template version on which to base the new version. The new version inherits the same launch parameters as the source version, except for parameters that you explicity specify. Snapshots applied to the O(block_device_mappings) are ignored when creating a new version unless they are explicitly included.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether the launch template should exist or not. Deleting specific versions of a launch template is not supported at this time.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A dictionary representing the tags to be applied to the resource. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified.

fields.label: template id
fields.description: The ID for the launch template, can be used for all cases except creating a new Launch Template. At least one of O(template_id) and O(template_name) must be specified.

fields.label: template name
fields.description: The template name. This must be unique in the region-account combination you are using. If no launch template exists with the specified name, a new launch template is created. If a launch template with the specified name already exists and the configuration has not changed, nothing happens. If a launch template with the specified name already exists and the configuration has changed, a new version of the launch template is created. At least one of O(template_id) and O(template_name) must be specified.

fields.label: user data
fields.description: The Base64-encoded user data to make available to the instance. For more information, see the Linux U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/user-data.html) and Windows U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/WindowsGuide/ec2-instance-metadata.html#instancedata-add-user-data) documentation on user-data.

fields.label: version description

fields.label: versions to delete

See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: region
fields.description: The AWS region to use. For global services such as IAM, Route53 and CloudFront, I(region) is ignored. The C(AWS_REGION) environment variable may also be used. See the Amazon AWS documentation for more information U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#ec2_region).

fields.label: secret key
fields.description: AWS secret access key. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or C(AWS_SECRET_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(secret_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_secret_access_key) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: session token
fields.description: AWS STS session token for use with temporary credentials. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SESSION_TOKEN) environment variable may also be used. The I(session_token) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: transit gateway ids
fields.description: A list of Transit Gateway IDs for which to gather information.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

modules.label: ec2_transit_gateway_vpc_attachment
modules.tooltip: Create and delete AWS Transit Gateway VPC attachments

fields.label: access key
fields.description: AWS access key ID. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) or C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(aws_access_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_access_key_id) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: appliance mode support
fields.description: Whether the attachment is configured for appliance mode. When appliance mode is enabled, Transit Gateway, using 4-tuples of an IP packet, selects a single Transit Gateway ENI in the Appliance VPC for the life of a flow to send traffic to.

fields.label: aws ca bundle
fields.description: The location of a CA Bundle to use when validating SSL certificates. The C(AWS_CA_BUNDLE) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: aws config
fields.description: A dictionary to modify the botocore configuration. Parameters can be found in the AWS documentation U(https://botocore.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/reference/config.html#botocore.config.Config).

fields.label: debug botocore endpoint logs
fields.description: Use a C(botocore.endpoint) logger to parse the unique (rather than total) C("resource:action") API calls made during a task, outputing the set to the resource_actions key in the task results. Use the C(aws_resource_action) callback to output to total list made during a playbook. The C(ANSIBLE_DEBUG_BOTOCORE_LOGS) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: dns support
fields.description: Whether DNS support is enabled.

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: id
fields.description: The ID of the Transit Gateway Attachment. When O(id) is not set, a search using O(transit_gateway) and O(name) will be performed. If multiple results are returned, the module will fail. At least one of O(name), O(transit_gateway) and O(id) must be provided.

fields.label: ipv6 support
fields.description: Whether IPv6 support is enabled.

fields.label: name
fields.description: The V(Name) tag of the Transit Gateway attachment. Providing both O(id) and O(name) will set the V(Name) tag on an existing attachment the matching O(id). Setting the V(Name) tag in O(tags) will also result in the V(Name) tag being updated. At least one of O(name), O(transit_gateway) and O(id) must be provided.

fields.label: profile
fields.description: A named AWS profile to use for authentication. See the AWS documentation for more information about named profiles U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-configure-profiles.html). The C(AWS_PROFILE) environment variable may also be used. The I(profile) option is mutually exclusive with the I(aws_access_key), I(aws_secret_key) and I(session_token) options.

fields.label: purge subnets
fields.description: If O(purge_subnets=true), existing subnets will be removed from the attachment as necessary to match exactly what is defined by O(subnets).

fields.label: purge tags
fields.description: If O(purge_tags=true) and O(tags) is set, existing tags will be purged from the resource to match exactly what is defined by O(tags) parameter. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified, even if O(purge_tags=True). Tag keys beginning with V(aws:) are reserved by Amazon and can not be modified.  As such they will be ignored for the purposes of the O(purge_tags) parameter.  See the Amazon documentation for more information U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws_tagging.html#tag-conventions).

fields.label: region
fields.description: The AWS region to use. For global services such as IAM, Route53 and CloudFront, I(region) is ignored. The C(AWS_REGION) environment variable may also be used. See the Amazon AWS documentation for more information U(http://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/rande.html#ec2_region).

fields.label: secret key
fields.description: AWS secret access key. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) or C(AWS_SECRET_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(secret_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive. The I(aws_secret_access_key) alias was added in release 5.1.0 for consistency with the AWS botocore SDK.

fields.label: session token
fields.description: AWS STS session token for use with temporary credentials. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_SESSION_TOKEN) environment variable may also be used. The I(session_token) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Create or remove the Transit Gateway attachment.

fields.label: subnets
fields.description: The ID of the subnets in which to create the transit gateway VPC attachment. Required when creating a new attachment.

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A dictionary representing the tags to be applied to the resource. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified.

fields.label: transit gateway
fields.description: The ID of the Transit Gateway that the attachment belongs to. When creating a new attachment, O(transit_gateway) must be provided. At least one of O(name), O(transit_gateway) and O(id) must be provided. O(transit_gateway) is an immutable setting and can not be updated on an existing attachment.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

fields.label: wait
fields.description: Whether to wait for the Transit Gateway attachment to reach the C(Available) or C(Deleted) state before the module returns.

fields.label: wait timeout
fields.description: Maximum time, in seconds, to wait for the Transit Gateway attachment to reach the expected state. Defaults to 600 seconds.

fields.label: delete on termination
fields.description: When set to C(true), the volume will be deleted upon instance termination.

fields.label: device name
fields.description: Device ID to override device mapping. Assumes /dev/sdf for Linux/UNIX and /dev/xvdf for Windows.

fields.label: encrypted
fields.description: Enable encryption at rest for this volume.

fields.label: endpoint url
fields.description: URL to connect to instead of the default AWS endpoints.  While this can be used to connection to other AWS-compatible services the amazon.aws and community.aws collections are only tested against AWS. The  C(AWS_URL) environment variable may also be used.

fields.label: id
fields.description: Volume ID if you wish to attach an existing volume (requires instance) or remove an existing volume.

fields.label: instance
fields.description: Instance ID if you wish to attach the volume. Set to V(None) to detach the volume.

fields.label: iops
fields.description: The provisioned IOPs you want to associate with this volume (integer).

fields.label: kms key id
fields.description: Specify the ID of the KMS key to use.

fields.label: modify volume
fields.description: The volume won't be modified unless this key is V(true).

fields.label: multi attach
fields.description: If set to V(true), Multi-Attach will be enabled when creating the volume. When you create a new volume, Multi-Attach is disabled by default. This parameter is supported with io1 and io2 volumes only.

fields.label: name
fields.description: Volume Name tag if you wish to attach an existing volume (requires instance).

fields.label: outpost arn
fields.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Outpost. If set, allows to create volume in an Outpost.

fields.label: snapshot
fields.description: Snapshot ID on which to base the volume.

fields.label: state
fields.description: Whether to ensure the volume is present or absent. O(state=list) was deprecated in release 1.1.0 and is no longer available with release 4.0.0. The V(list) functionality has been moved to a dedicated module M(amazon.aws.ec2_vol_info).

fields.label: tags
fields.description: A dictionary representing the tags to be applied to the resource. If the O(tags) parameter is not set then tags will not be modified.

fields.label: throughput
fields.description: Volume throughput in MB/s. This parameter is only valid for gp3 volumes. Valid range is from 125 to 2000.

fields.label: validate certs
fields.description: When set to C(false), SSL certificates will not be validated for communication with the AWS APIs. Setting I(validate_certs=false) is strongly discouraged, as an alternative, consider setting I(aws_ca_bundle) instead.

fields.label: volume initialization rate
fields.description: Rate at which to initiliaze the volume from a snapshot.

fields.label: volume size
fields.description: Size of volume (in GiB) to create.

fields.label: volume type
fields.description: Type of EBS volume; V(standard) (magnetic), V(gp2) (SSD), V(gp3) (SSD), V(io1) (Provisioned IOPS), V(io2) (Provisioned IOPS), V(st1) (Throughput Optimized HDD), V(sc1) (Cold HDD). V(standard) is the old EBS default and continues to remain the Ansible default for backwards compatibility.

fields.label: zone
fields.description: Zone in which to create the volume, if unset uses the zone the instance is in (if set).

fields.label: egress
fields.description: A list of rules for outgoing traffic. Each rule must be specified as a list. Each rule may contain the rule number (integer 1-32766), protocol (one of ['tcp', 'udp', 'icmp', 'ipv6-icmp', '-1', 'all']), the rule action ('allow' or 'deny') the CIDR of the IPv4 or IPv6 network range to allow or deny, the ICMP type (-1 means all types), the ICMP code (-1 means all codes), the last port in the range for TCP or UDP protocols, and the first port in the range for TCP or UDP protocols. See examples.

fields.label: lookup
fields.description: Look up route table by either O(tags) or by O(route_table_id). If O(lookup=tag) and O(tags) is not specified then no lookup for an existing route table is performed and a new route table will be created. When using O(lookup=tag), multiple matches being found will result in a failure and no changes will be made. To change the tags of a route table use O(lookup=id). O(vpc_id) must be specified when O(lookup=tag).

The value of V(dest) is used for the destination match. It may be a IPv4 CIDR block or a IPv6 CIDR block. If V(gateway_id) is specified, you can refer to the VPC's IGW by using the value V(igw). Routes are required for present states.

fields.label: access key
fields.description: AWS access key ID. See the AWS documentation for more information about access tokens U(https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-sec-cred-types.html#access-keys-and-secret-access-keys). The C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) or C(AWS_ACCESS_KEY) environment variables may also be used in decreasing order of preference. The I(aws_access_key) and I(profile) options are mutually exclusive.
